title: Dioscorea herradurensis
taxonomy: Dioscorea herradurensis is a species of yam in the family Dioscoreaceae. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Dioscorea. Dioscorea herradurensis is native to Mexico and is part of the Dioscoreales order. This species is closely related to other Dioscorea species, which are commonly known as yams or wild yams. Further research is needed to confirm the specific classification of Dioscorea herradurensis within the larger plant kingdom.

intro: Dioscorea herradurensis, also known as the "Heart-Shaped Yam," is a species of yam plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to regulate blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Dioscorea herradurensis include diosgenin, a steroidal saponin with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as other saponins and alkaloids. In traditional herbal medicine, Dioscorea herradurensis is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the production of dietary supplements and food products.
